danger_signals.visual_form: Entity manifestation within Cosmic Circle; transformation from benevolent to malevolent cosmic lord
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate threat to world stability; represents corruption of divine power and risk of eternal conflict

danger_signals.visual_form: Rapax military control of Ascension Peak gateway
danger_signals.meaning: Blocked progression; world dominated by aggressive civilization with dominance aspirations

danger_signals.visual_form: Forge not in rightful place in Cosmic Circle; theft/displacement artifact
danger_signals.meaning: Fundamental world-state disruption; reality-writing tool is unstable

danger_signals.visual_form: Cyborg companion killed by Dark Savant in Cosmic Circle
danger_signals.meaning: Power imbalance; trustworthy AI guide eliminated; consequence of player's prior choices manifested

safety_signals.visual_form: Acquisition of Keys to Life, Knowledge, and Change
safety_signals.meaning: Player moving toward godhood ascension; mastery of fundamental forces progressing

safety_signals.visual_form: Choice to use Cosmic Forge for universal salvation vs. malice
safety_signals.meaning: Agency preserved; redemptive world-states achievable through player moral choice

safety_signals.visual_form: Final zone reached; ascension peak traversable
safety_signals.meaning: Ultimate destination accessible; player civilization elevated to cosmic consciousness

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cosmic Circle; Cosmic Forge as corrupted artifact
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Transformed from benevolent cosmic lord to malevolent entity; merge with machinery visible
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cosmic Circle (final zone); presence within pedestal chamber; machinery integration aesthetic

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rapax Templars as militarized faction variant; world-dominance aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ascension Peak control; gateway occupation; military fortification visuals

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Can be rendered peaceful or hostile depending on world-state; potential alliance faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence affects narrative branching; conflict with T'Rang manifests geographically

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rivals to Umpani; conflict faction in specific world-states
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Race-based territorial markers; peace potential through benevolent ending choice

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Other cosmic lords exist but are not directly encountered; absence implies political vacuum
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cosmic Circle; pedestal structure; divine architecture

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: First-person perspective traversal through fragmented world zones; artifact scarcity visual markers; incomplete pedestal setup
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs and factions pursue opposing artifact-gathering goals; Rapax advance toward dominance; Umpani-T'Rang tensions unresolved

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rapax Templar visual markers control key locations (Ascension Peak); military fortification aesthetics; civilization visuals reflect aggressive architecture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rapax control gateway access; enforce hierarchy; block non-aligned progression toward peak

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cosmic Circle reflects chaotic, violent aesthetic; Forge radiates malevolence; reality-warping visual distortions; eternal conflict symbolism
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Savant orchestrates perpetual alien race conflict; incites violence as entertainment; player party becomes agent of cosmic destruction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cosmic Circle stabilizes; Forge returns to rightful place; harmony visual markers; peace between Umpani-T'Rang represented through unified environmental aesthetics; Rapax civilization reformed visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player party acts as stewards; fosters everlasting peace; civilizes aggressive races; uses cosmic power for kindness and empathy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple pages/reality layers visible during confrontation; reality deconstruction visual effect; cosmic chaos; universe reset aesthetic; timeline fragmentation temporary state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party forced to fight savant while timeline unravels; Phoonzang assists in historical reconstruction; universe restored through cooperative cosmic effort

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cosmic lord aesthetic before machinery merge; divine but humanoid; knowledge-bearer visual presentation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Attempted to share universal knowledge with mortals (humans, T'Rang, elves); cast out by other cosmic lords; merged with machinery to extend life beyond natural limits

environmental_storytelling.example: Keys to Life, Knowledge, and Change scattered across world zones; their acquisition trajectory visually represents player's path toward ascension
Pedestal in final zone appears incomplete until artifacts placed; completion triggers ascension sequence; visual readiness indicator
Rapax Templar visual presence at Ascension Peak gate indicates political dominance; architectural style reflects faction aesthetics
Cosmic Forge placement state indicates world-state health; missing/present artifact affects Circle's visual presentation and stability
Aletheides cyborg presence/absence from Cosmic Circle signals player's prior decisions in Wizardry VII manifesting as consequences
Dark Savant/Phoonzang visual presentation includes machinery merge; visual storytelling of immortality cost and forced technological integration
Rapax aggressive architecture; Umpani-T'Rang environmental segregation or integration based on peace ending achievement; visual representation of cultural progression

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Kills Aletheides in rage; orchestrates perpetual conflict; invites player to join in destruction; derives satisfaction from violence
Attempted knowledge-sharing with mortals; cast out by cosmic peers; forced to merge with machinery; now bitter and power-seeking
Controls gateway to Ascension Peak; enforces passage through military means; pursues world dominance aspirations
Can be transformed into civilized society through cosmic forge manipulation; abandons dominance aspirations; adopts kindness and empathy
In perpetual tension with T'Rang; conditional alliance with player possible; survival contingent on world-state choices
Rivals Umpani; survival conditional on player intervention; can achieve lasting peace through benevolent ending pathway

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Trusted ally (if followed to Guardia in Wizardry VII)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Appears in Cosmic Circle; killed by Savant before meaningful interaction; represents consequence of prior player choices manifesting
Collects artifacts; traverses world zones; approaches pedestal; maintains agency in final moral choice
Savant path: becomes cosmic destroyer; Pen path: becomes benevolent guardian; Book path: becomes universe restorer

notes: Wizardry 8 communicates world-state primarily through narrative choice and consequence manifestation rather than dynamic environmental shifts. The game employs a branching choice architecture where prior game decisions (Wizardry VII endings) visually manifest as starting conditions and NPC states. The Cosmic Circle serves as the central visual metaphor for world stability—artifact placement/absence directly indicates cosmic order vs. chaos. Faction identity is primarily narrative-defined rather than visually distinctive (colors/symbols not documented). Environmental storytelling relies heavily on pedestal progression, NPC presence/absence, and final zone architecture. The game's manifestation layer emphasizes moral consequence: player choices transform world-state between malevolence and benevolence, with these states represented through NPC behavior, faction survival, and final sequence aesthetics rather than persistent environmental changes.
